text: An upgrade
A suite
Courtesy
The concierge
Room service
Housekeeping
A wake-up call
Late check-out
A discrepancy
To escalate a complaint
A vacancy
Valet parking
To accommodate
Could you accommodate an early check-in?
She asked whether I had stayed there before.
I asked where the lift was.
I asked housekeeping to send up some towels.
The receptionist told me not to leave valuables in the room.
Good evening. I have a reservation under Adamo, but there seems to be a discrepancy. I booked a double room, and the confirmation shows a single.
I am so sorry about that. Let me check. You are quite right. As a courtesy, I will upgrade you to a suite at no extra charge.
That is very kind, thank you. While I have you, could you arrange a wake-up call for six, and is late check-out possible tomorrow?
Of course. I have set the wake-up call, and I can offer late check-out until one o clock. Would you like me to ask the concierge about a taxi as well?
Yes, please, a taxi to the airport for half past two. And one last thing, does the suite have room service in the evening?
It does, until midnight. Everything is arranged. If anything is not right, just call the front desk and we will accommodate you.

Good evening, this is a message from the front desk for the guest in room four-one-two. Earlier you asked whether late check-out was possible, and I am pleased to confirm that we can offer it until two o'clock at no extra charge. You also asked the concierge to arrange a taxi to the airport; that is now booked for half past two. Finally, regarding the noise you reported last night, the manager has asked me to apologise and has upgraded your room to a quiet suite on the top floor for your final evening. Please do let us know if there is anything else we can do.

Honestly, that hotel turned a tricky night into a lovely stay. When I checked in, the receptionist asked whether I had stayed with them before, and she told me to keep the welcome drink voucher for the rooftop bar. There was a small discrepancy on my bill, so I asked the manager to look into it, and within minutes he had removed the charge and added a courtesy breakfast. I learned something on this trip: if you stay calm and explain the problem clearly, most hotels will go out of their way to accommodate you. A good hotel is not the one where nothing goes wrong, but the one that puts it right.
